steps.title: Understand standard deviation and identify the key difference
steps.content: Both groups surveyed 2,500 men and reported the same mean height, 186 cm. That means the center of the two distributions is the same. The useful shortcut is to ignore the matching mean and compare only the standard deviations, because standard deviation measures spread around the mean.

steps.title: Compare the standard deviations
steps.content: Group A has standard deviation $12.5$, and Group B has standard deviation $19.1$. Since $19.1\gt 12.5$, Group B has the larger spread. The table does not give the tallest participant or either median, so the other choices cannot be known from the information given.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The correct answer says the heights in Group B had a larger spread than the heights in Group A. That is exactly what the larger standard deviation shows, so C is correct.
